Decision Title: Use n8n as orchestrator with Next.js dashboard and SQLite on NAS
Context Summary: Non-developer team; needed maintainable visual tooling on existing QNAP NAS infrastructure
Decision Summary: n8n (self-hosted Docker) + Next.js dashboard (Kiranism fork) + SQLite (better-sqlite3) + Tailscale
Rationale Summary: Best balance of no-code orchestration, dashboard flexibility, and local-first data on existing hardware
Outcome Notes: Superseded by ADR-0011 — QNAP admin access lost; cloud-first replaces self-hosted

Context Summary: Four of six upload platforms have API barriers, no APIs, or fragile automation paths
Decision Summary: Phase 1 = YouTube + Dropbox automated only; four platforms manual-with-assist; Rumble/Signiant Phase 2; Fireside/StreamHoster Phase 3
Rationale Summary: 8+ weeks to automate high-risk platforms; ROI too low for Phase 1; manual workflow is first-class citizen per ADR-0003

Decision Title: Dashboard is a tracking system that sometimes does automated uploads
Context Summary: Two framings possible: automation-primary vs tracking-primary
Decision Summary: Tracking system is primary; automation is one feature per platform; manual workflows are first-class
Rationale Summary: Avoids making manual feel like failure; identical DB schema across all platforms; switching is config change not redesign

Decision Title: Two-NAS topology — QNAP3 as primary automation host
Context Summary: Discovered two QNAP units during Week 1: DRM-QNAP3 (10.2.2.3) and DRM-QNAP5 (10.2.2.5)
Decision Summary: QNAP3 = primary automation host for all Docker services; QNAP5 = storage peer only
Rationale Summary: Needed canonical home for all automation; QNAP3 chosen as more powerful unit; QNAP5 for redundancy only
Outcome Notes: Superseded — QNAP admin access blocked by security incident 2026-05-19

Context Summary: Master files land on both QNAP3 and QNAP5; which does Chokidar watch?
Decision Summary: Deferred — requires QNAP admin access and editor/Miryam workflow discussion
Rationale Summary: Cannot decide without (a) full infra inventory and (b) team workflow conversation; recorded to avoid losing question
Outcome Notes: Superseded — QNAP no longer hosts automation; cloud sync client is new trigger

Decision Title: No QNAP admin access — cloud-first architecture with Notion database
Context Summary: Security incident 2026-05-19: QNAP credentials exposed in chat; IT revoked all QNAP admin access; David requires isolated/controlled connections only
Decision Summary: Notion replaces SQLite; n8n.cloud replaces self-hosted n8n; sync client on Edit Bay Mac Mini watches SMB share (read-only); QNAP = file source only
Rationale Summary: Eliminates need for QNAP admin; aligns with David's security requirements; Notion provides team visibility with no maintenance; simpler and safer
Outcome Notes: Open: which sync client (Dropbox vs Google Drive); Notion schema design (this session)
